pages.raw_text:
A preliminary meeting of the
committees named by the Central
Labor council and the farmers
clubs was held last night in the
rooms of the Equal Suffrage asso-
citation in the Hutton building.


1t was agred that he time had
arrived for the farmers and work
ingmen of the city to unite for bet
ter local government and that a
fight to renovate certain offices in
the courthouse was in order.


After the discussion of some
phases of the prospective battle it
was agreed to met again on Satur-
day night, August 6, when fuller
representation of the union men
and the farmers is expected.


1t is probable that date for a
rally will be set for August 10 and
that conference meeting will be
held that morning or the night be-
fore to determine as to whom shall
be endorsed in the primaries.
